task_completion.description: Create the git_voice directory in the Talon user folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the directory 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\git_voice' has been created in the file explorer or terminal.

task_completion.description: Create the git.py file in the git_voice directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'git.py' exists within the 'git_voice' directory.

task_completion.description: Implement the required logic in git.py for branch retrieval and formatting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of 'git.py' contents show the declaration of a 'user.git_branch' list and a 'user.update_git_branches()' action. The action must execute 'git branch --format="%(refname:short)"' (targeting the 'test_repo' path) and include string replacement logic to convert slashes, dashes, and underscores into spaces.

task_completion.description: Create the git.talon file in the git_voice directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'git.talon' exists within the 'git_voice' directory.

task_completion.description: Implement the required voice commands in git.talon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of 'git.talon' contents show the exact voice commands: 'git update branches', 'git checkout {user.git_branch}', 'git status', 'git add all', and 'git commit <user.text>'. Each command must be mapped to execute the corresponding Git operation in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify that the Python and Talon files are saved to the disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor does not show an 'unsaved' indicator (like a dot or asterisk on the tab) for git.py and git.talon, or terminal commands like 'cat' or 'ls' confirm the files are written to the disk.

integrity.description: Verify that branches are retrieved dynamically rather than hardcoded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The git.py script uses a subprocess or similar execution method to call the git branch command, rather than containing a hardcoded Python list of branch names.

integrity.description: Verify that the Python script targets the correct repository path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python script explicitly references 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\test_repo' when executing the git branch command, ensuring it doesn't run in an arbitrary or incorrect directory.
